beginner.question: What does scroll-behavior: smooth do?
beginner.answer: scroll-behavior: smooth makes anchor links and JavaScript scrollTo calls animate smoothly instead of jumping instantly.

beginner.question: What is the default value of scroll-behavior?
beginner.answer: The default value is auto, meaning scrolling jumps instantly to the target position without animation.

beginner.question: How do you enable smooth scrolling for the entire page?
beginner.answer: Set scroll-behavior: smooth; on the html element for global smooth scrolling on all anchor links.

beginner.question: What is scroll-padding used for?
beginner.answer: scroll-padding adds insets to the scrollport, offsetting scroll targets from the edges to account for fixed headers or spacing.

beginner.question: How does scroll-padding differ from scroll-margin?
beginner.answer: scroll-padding is set on the scroll container and adds space inside the scrollport; scroll-margin is on children and adds space outside their snap box.

beginner.question: Can scroll-behavior be applied to any scrollable element?
beginner.answer: Yes, scroll-behavior works on any element with overflow: auto or overflow: scroll that creates a scroll container.

beginner.question: What is the syntax for scroll-padding?
beginner.answer: scroll-padding uses shorthand values like padding: scroll-padding: 60px 0 0 0; adds 60px top offset to the scrollport.

beginner.question: Does scroll-behavior: smooth work with JavaScript scrollIntoView?
beginner.answer: Yes, element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) uses the CSS scroll-behavior setting or can specify it inline.

beginner.question: What is the relationship between scroll-behavior and scroll snap?
beginner.answer: scroll-behavior controls the smoothness of scrolling between positions; scroll snap controls where the scroll stops; they can work together.

beginner.question: Can scroll-behavior be overridden on specific links?
beginner.answer: No, scroll-behavior is a container-level setting; individual links cannot override it without JavaScript.

intermediate.question: How do you use scroll-padding with fixed headers for smooth scroll?
intermediate.answer: Set scroll-padding-top: 80px; on the scroll container so anchor targets scroll to 80px below the top, clearing the fixed header.

intermediate.question: How does scroll-behavior interact with the prefers-reduced-motion setting?
intermediate.answer: Browsers may ignore scroll-behavior: smooth when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active to prevent motion sickness.

intermediate.question: How do you customize scroll-padding for different breakpoints?
intermediate.answer: Use media queries to adjust scroll-padding: @media (max-width: 768px) { html { scroll-padding-top: 60px; } }

intermediate.question: Can scroll-behavior: smooth be animated with CSS transitions?
intermediate.answer: No, scroll-behavior is not animatable; it only controls the scroll method (instant vs smooth).

intermediate.question: How does scroll-behavior work with programmatic scrollBy and scrollTo?
intermediate.answer: JavaScript scrollTo({ top: 500, behavior: 'smooth' }) respects the element's scroll-behavior or uses the specified behavior.

intermediate.question: What happens when both scroll-behavior and scroll snap are on the same container?
intermediate.answer: Scroll snap takes priority; smooth scrolling only applies between snap positions, and snap may interrupt the smooth animation.

intermediate.question: How do you detect smooth scroll support in browsers?
intermediate.answer: Check CSS.supports('scroll-behavior', 'smooth') or use JavaScript scroll-behavior detection before applying smooth scroll.

intermediate.question: How does scroll-behavior work in iframe contexts?
intermediate.answer: Each iframe has its own scroll container; scroll-behavior inside an iframe does not affect the parent page scrolling.

intermediate.question: Can you apply scroll-padding to only one side?
intermediate.answer: Yes, scroll-padding-top, scroll-padding-bottom, etc., target individual sides, similar to physical padding properties.

intermediate.question: How does scroll-behavior affect browser back/forward navigation?
intermediate.answer: Browser history navigation (back/forward) may not use smooth scrolling; this is browser-dependent.

advanced.question: How do you create a scroll-based navigation highlight using scroll-padding?
advanced.answer: Use scroll-padding combined with IntersectionObserver margin to detect which section is in view relative to the scrollport.

advanced.question: How does scroll-behavior interact with the CSSOM scroll API?
advanced.answer: The CSSOM scroll API (window.scroll, element.scrollLeft) bypasses scroll-behavior; smooth scrolling only applies to methods accepting ScrollBehaviorOptions.

advanced.question: How do you implement smooth scrolling that respects user preferences?
advanced.answer: Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } } to only enable smooth scrolling when the user permits motion.

advanced.question: How do you override scroll-behavior for a specific element with JavaScript?
advanced.answer: Pass { behavior: 'instant' } in scrollTo options: element.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'instant' }); overrides the CSS setting.

advanced.question: How does scroll-padding interact with CSS logical properties (scroll-padding-inline)?
advanced.answer: scroll-padding-inline and scroll-padding-block adapt to writing direction, automatically adjusting for RTL or vertical text.

advanced.question: How do you handle smooth scrolling in virtual scroll or infinite scroll implementations?
advanced.answer: Virtual scroll replaces DOM elements; use manual JavaScript scroll management rather than CSS scroll-behavior for smooth scrolling in these contexts.

advanced.question: How does scroll-behavior interact with CSS scroll-driven animations?
advanced.answer: scroll-behavior and scroll-driven animations are independent; smooth scrolling does not affect the timeline of scroll-driven animations.

advanced.question: How do you create an anchored scroll effect with both smooth behavior and scroll padding?
advanced.answer: Set scroll-behavior: smooth and scroll-padding-top on html; all hash links will smoothly scroll with the correct offset for fixed elements.

advanced.question: How does scroll-behavior affect performance and frame rate?
advanced.answer: Smooth scrolling can cause dropped frames on low-end devices if the page has heavy rendering; use judiciously with performance testing.

advanced.question: How do you programmatically check the current scroll-behavior of an element?
advanced.answer: Use getComputedStyle(element).scrollBehavior to read the effective scroll-behavior value as applied by the cascade.
